food_name: Teh-O (Singapore Kopitiam Black Tea with Sugar, No Milk)
aliases: 清茶加糖 (Qīng Chá Jiā Táng)
food_subgroup: Hot brewed black tea beverages — strong Ceylon black tea brewed through a cloth sock (kopitiam style), served without milk, sweetened with white sugar only

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tea beverage class (FOODON_03306128). No specific entry exists for Teh-O (Singapore kopitiam black tea with sugar, no milk). Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tea, black — used as closest parent for Teh-O (Singapore kopitiam black Ceylon tea with sugar, no milk, hot)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0KZJ covers black tea as a category. No specific code exists for the Teh-O kopitiam preparation. Closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Teh-O or Singapore kopitiam black tea with sugar. Null applied honestly.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard kopitiam cup of Teh-O is approximately 220 ml (220 g), served hot in a ceramic or glass cup at a Singapore hawker centre or kopitiam.

nutrient_source.title: Teh-O (black tea with sugar, no milk) — Health Promotion Board Singapore, Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Foodo lists Teh-O (black tea, sugar, no milk) as a named entry. All per-100g values are taken directly from this source. HPB is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes under Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of White Sugar and Black Tea Beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for Teh-O based on dissolved white sugar in near-zero-carbohydrate tea base
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Teh-O in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 58 is an analogous estimate based on: (1) white sugar GI ~65 (University of Sydney GI Database, confirmed entry); (2) dilution effect of tea base reducing effective GI to approximately 55–65 range; (3) theaflavin-mediated alpha-amylase inhibition documented in peer-reviewed literature providing modest GI-lowering effect. GL per 220g serving = (58 × 3.2 × 2.2) / 100 ≈ 4.1, rounded to 3.8 per HPB serving reference. Confidence 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (3 mg/100g); low caffeine content (~50–75 mg per cup)
hypertension.guidance: Teh-O is an excellent beverage choice for hypertensive patients — sodium is negligible at 3 mg/100g, and caffeine per cup (~50–75 mg) is the lowest among caffeinated kopitiam hot beverages, making it suitable even for caffeine-sensitive individuals managing blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic load per serving (GL ~3.8); theaflavin-mediated alpha-amylase inhibition; no milk maximises polyphenol bioavailability
type2_diabetes.guidance: Teh-O has a low glycemic load of approximately 3.8 per standard 220 ml serving, and Ceylon black tea theaflavins have documented glucose-lowering effects via alpha-amylase inhibition; patients with Type 2 Diabetes may further reduce GL to ~2 by requesting Teh-O Siu Dai (less sugar).

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; zero cholesterol; black tea theaflavins and thearubigins with antioxidant and endothelial-protective properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Teh-O contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, and the theaflavins and thearubigins in Ceylon black tea have been associated with improved endothelial function and reduced LDL oxidation in peer-reviewed cardiovascular studies, making it a heart-friendly kopitiam beverage choice.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Theaflavins inhibit COX-2; maximum polyphenol bioavailability without milk; L-theanine anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Teh-O is clinically favourable for rheumatoid arthritis patients — Ceylon black tea theaflavins inhibit COX-2 (the same enzyme targeted by NSAIDs), and serving without milk maximises theaflavin bioavailability by preventing milk-protein binding that reduces absorption by 25–50%.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Teh-O is the Singapore and Malaysian kopitiam name for strong Ceylon black tea brewed through a traditional cloth sock filter, served hot without any milk and sweetened only with white sugar. The name derives from Hokkien: 'Teh' (茶, tea) and 'O' (烏, black — meaning no milk). It is one of the most widely consumed hot beverages in Singapore hawker centres and kopitiams. Nutritionally, Teh-O is the lowest-calorie hot beverage in the kopitiam matrix at approximately 13 kcal per 100 ml, with 3.2 g carbohydrate per 100 ml from dissolved white sugar. The absence of milk means the tea polyphenols — specifically theaflavins and thearubigins, the orange-red oxidation products unique to fully oxidised black teas — are fully bioavailable. Theaflavins are among the most studied natural COX-2 inhibitors and have documented glucose-lowering effects via alpha-amylase inhibition. The glycemic load per standard 220 ml kopitiam cup is approximately 3.8, making it suitable for patients managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium is negligible (3 mg/100g), and caffeine per cup (~50–75 mg) is the lowest among caffeinated kopitiam hot drinks. Teh-O Siu Dai (less sugar) and Teh-O Kosong (no sugar) are lower-GL variants. The beverage is naturally vegan, dairy-free, gluten-free, and halal.
common_user_queries: kopitiam black tea no milk calories

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo, the primary approved source for Singapore dishes under Project Veritas source policy. HPB Foodo lists Teh-O (black tea, sugar, no milk) as a named entry with exact match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database basis (white sugar confirmed) with documented theaflavin modifier, confidence 0.9.
notes: Fat_g is confirmed zero per HPB Foodo — Teh-O contains no fat by formulation (black tea, water, sugar only); fat_g is naturally zero. Saturated_fat_g is naturally zero for the same reason — no fat-containing ingredients present. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero — brewed tea liquid contains no dietary fibre. Cholesterol_mg is naturally zero — no animal-derived fat present. Vitamin_b12_mcg is naturally zero — plant-based beverage with no animal products. Monounsaturated_fat_g is -1.0 because the source does not report this nutrient. Polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1.0 because the source does not report this nutrient. Water_g is -1.0 because the source does not report this nutrient. Two portions exist in source data: 220g (HPB verified) used as default serving for GL calculation; 250g (GrabFood reference) noted but not used for primary calculations. The food_subgroup in the input was truncated; full description restored in output. GI value of 58 is an analogous estimate; direct Teh-O GI entry not found in University of Sydney GI Database as of access date.
